<answer>
**Summary of the Query:**
The query seeks to determine which German composer's work, specifically "Der Lindberghflug," was performed by the Pacific Mozart Ensemble in 2002.

**Necessary Skills and Tools:**
1. **Research Skills:**
   - **Explanation:** To identify the German composer associated with "Der Lindberghflug" and confirm the performance details.
2. **Web Search Skills:**
   - **Explanation:** Utilizing search engines or specific databases to find information about the Pacific Mozart Ensemble's performances and the compositions they have performed.
3. **Information Extraction:**
   - **Explanation:** Gleaning specific details from search results, such as the composer’s name and the year of performance.

**Tools:**
1. **Ground_Google_Search_Tool:**
   - **Explanation:** This tool is suitable for conducting a broad search across the web to find relevant information about the Pacific Mozart Ensemble and their performances in 2002.
2. **Wikipedia_RAG_Search_Tool:**
   - **Explanation:** This tool can be used to look up information on the Pacific Mozart Ensemble and their performances, particularly focusing on their 2002 performances.
3. **Web_RAG_Search_Tool:**
   - **Explanation:** Useful for retrieving specific information from a relevant website if the exact performance details are available on a specific site.

**Additional Considerations:**
- Ensure the information from multiple sources is cross-verified for accuracy.
- The query may require breaking down into smaller sub-queries to gather comprehensive information.
</answer>

- **Memory (Tools Used & Results):** {'Action Step 1': {'tool_name': 'Ground_Google_Search_Tool', 'sub_goal': 'Find information about the Pacific Mozart Ensemble's performances in 2002, specifically focusing on the performance of "Der Lindberghflug."', 'command': 'execution = tool.execute(query="Pacific Mozart Ensemble performed which German composer's Der Lindberghflug in 2002?")', 'result': ['The Pacific Mozart Ensemble performed "Der Lindberghflug" by the German composer Kurt Weill in 2002.[1](https://imslp.org/wiki/Der_Lindberghflug_(Weill,_Kurt)), [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kurt_Weill), [3](https://www.scribd.com/document/503803204/Kurt-Weill-Der-Lindberghflug), [4](https://daniels-orchestral.com/kurt-weill/der-lindberghflug-the-lindbergh-flight/), [5](https://www.universaledition.com/en/Der-Lindberghflug-Der-Ozeanflug/P0012373)

Kurt Weill (1900-1950) was a German-born American composer, best known for his collaborations with Bertolt Brecht. "Der Lindberghflug," a cantata with text by Bertolt Brecht, was composed in 1929[1](https://imslp.org/wiki/Der_Lindberghflug_(Weill,_Kurt)), [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kurt_Weill), [3](https://www.scribd.com/document/503803204/Kurt-Weill-Der-Lindberghflug). It was originally conceived as a radio play and cantata, with an initial version including music by both Weill and Paul Hindemith. Weill later produced a version solely with his music[1](https://imslp.org/wiki/Der_Lindberghflug_(Weill,_Kurt)), [3](https://www.scribd.com/document/503803204/Kurt-Weill-Der-Lindberghflug). The work tells the story of Charles Lindbergh's historic transatlantic flight[1](https://imslp.org/wiki/Der_Lindberghflug_(Weill,_Kurt)), [4](https://daniels-orchestral.com/kurt-weill/der-lindberghflug-the-lindbergh-flight/).']}}

result:
The Pacific Mozart Ensemble performed "Der Lindberghflug" by the German composer Kurt Weill in 2002.[1](https://imslp.org/wiki/Der_Lindberghflug_(Weill,_Kurt)), [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kurt_Weill), [3](https://www.scribd.com/document/503803204/Kurt-Weill-Der-Lindberghflug), [4](https://daniels-orchestral.com/kurt-weill/der-lindberghflug-the-lindbergh-flight/), [5](https://www.universaledition.com/en/Der-Lindberghflug-Der-Ozeanflug/P0012373)

Kurt Weill (1900-1950) was a German-born American composer, best known for his collaborations with Bertolt Brecht. "Der Lindberghflug," a cantata with text by Bertolt Brecht, was composed in 1929[1](https://imslp.org/wiki/Der_Lindberghflug_(Weill,_Kurt)), [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kurt_Weill), [3](https://www.scribd.com/document/503803204/Kurt-Weill-Der-Lindberghflug). It was originally conceived as a radio play and cantata, with an initial version including music by both Weill and Paul Hindemith. Weill later produced a version solely with his music[1](https://imslp.org/wiki/Der_Lindberghflug_(Weill,_Kurt)), [3](https://www.scribd.com/document/503803204/Kurt-Weill-Der-Lindberghflug). The work tells the story of Charles Lindbergh's historic transatlantic flight[1](https://imslp.org/wiki/Der_Lindberghflug_(Weill,_Kurt)), [4](https://daniels-orchestral.com/kurt-weill/der-lindberghflug-the-lindbergh-flight/).

direct_output:
<answer>
The Pacific Mozart Ensemble performed "Der Lindberghflug" by the German composer Kurt Weill in 2002. Kurt Weill was a German-born American composer known for his collaborations with Bertolt Brecht. "Der Lindberghflug" is a cantata with text by Bertolt Brecht, originally conceived as a radio play and cantata, telling the story of Charles Lindbergh's historic transatlantic flight.
</answer>
